Herbalist's Spade  
 

Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 2H Blunt Atk Delay: 43
DMG: 39 Dmg Bonus: 150
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
Effect: Paralyzing Earth (Combat, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 49
WT: 3.0 Size: SMALL
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

battle cleric
QuoteReply
# Sep 20 2004 at 8:26 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592343673941
161 posts
would be a good battle cleric item... they get root, but t root back up full heal. and go back in makes them almost more scary then a pally.
▲ Page top
Bards can use it
QuoteReply
# Mar 30 2004 at 11:11 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591799667709
1 post
Ugg some people have no clue.. As to the people who say this is useless for Bard.. Get a clue. Even though it is combat and Bard have only 2hb(1) this weapon will still proc for a Bard.. You can proc on a miss and your attack rating or attack speed have nothing to do with proc rate.. It has been proven at the SteelWarriors that proc rate is independant and with a 305DEX you will proc 2 times per minute regardless of skill..

So this weapon is useful for Bards.. But at level 49 (when it procs) I don't see many Bards wanting to stand toe to toe with a mob for it to proc when we can just run away and chant..
▲ Page top
Spade
QuoteReply
# Mar 16 2004 at 3:31 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757700860
Scholar
49 posts
Dropped for me last night. The wizened herb collector was not weilding it, so it was a pleasant surprise when I saw the herbalist's spade as his drop.
▲ Page top
Better than taunt
QuoteReply
# May 30 2003 at 11:50 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592257362884
18 posts
This weapon is darn near godly in the hands of a warrior who has trouble gaining taunt from those stun casting pallies and SK's. Once the root takes effect the mob will hit whoever is closest to it. Think of it as 100% aggro all the time, soon as it procs just get belly to belly with the mob and NOONE is taking taunt from you, then if your alomost going to die just step back and the next closest person has the mobs attention. Nice ratio too and it procs 5-8 times per fight on average. There are many other uses for this weapon too, ghetto mezzing adds comes to mind. Well worth paying 35k for it and best of all its all/all
▲ Page top
Proc Level ?
QuoteReply
# Feb 19 2003 at 2:16 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591785403365
Scholar
105 posts
I know the effect is a High level spell (my 54 necro uses PE to Plant a mob when needed)So that said I am really curious as to what level this thing will Proc.My thinking here is that the spell is of such a high level that the proc is probably also.PE(effect) is very reliable as a great root.I use mainly to stack Dots on the mob before allowing pet to engage.However,this weapon is of particular intrest to me as I also play a Beastlord.This may be the only way for a BL to be able to plant a mob.This weapon alone would make training up 2hb a must for me.Well if anyone has any real first hand knowledge of when it procs(if even if your high level)please let me know.Thanks
▲ Page top

QuoteReply

Scholar

the one thing about this item is paralyzing earth lol.. sure nice proc.. but it roots for 3min lol.. which means fight to death

Herbalists Spade
QuoteReply
# May 13 2002 at 12:21 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591776410526
Sage
280 posts
Seeing this as a 54 shaman who still melees a bit makes me drool--its about 20% better than the fungus coated tarmok staff which i have now--not even counting the proc. Im sure its fairly hard to get given its ratio, but I'll be leaving the hornets nest in BW to get one of these soon. For those who dont think a shaman can still melee dark blue mobs at this level, come partner with me sometime on Luclin server...you will be a bit suprised. Anyway a nice weapon to whoever gets it.
54 Ogre Battleshaman on Luclin Server--Aggrok
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Apr 30 2002 at 9:48 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Doesn't matter that you don't have a 2hb skill. If your 1hb is high, and you can use this, it goes off that skill. Still an obvious Monk item. :p
RE: 2hb
QuoteReply
# Jun 29 2002 at 12:01 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1595780129657
294 posts
What're you smokin', cuz I sure want some...

If you don't have 2hb skill.. get some, cuz if the weapon used 2hb, the weap will go off you're TWO-Hand Blunt skill... if it didn't, you could dual wield with it.

*quickly beats his head on the desk*
Where does EQ FIND these ppl, I swear. :P lol

Jarholi Scalefist

Can anyone verify that this is a TRIGGERED spell and not a melee proc? if it is triggered, this is a mages WET DREAM! Everyone knows we got jipped out of the root line of spells, using our earth pet's 10-second root that doesn't stick as an excuse.

this is n awsome wepon i feel, but only thig is how can it be all al when bards can't use 2h anything, just a thought

QuoteReply
# Feb 24 2002 at 9:57 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592253690003
4 posts
Bards can use a few 2h weapons, they just don't get the skill. So while a bard can use this, he wouldn't have any skill in 2hb and it would be useless.

RE: Hmm
QuoteReply
# Apr 10 2002 at 9:38 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591776103303
81 posts
its not the weapon thats useless for melee...>:)
▲ Page top
RE: Hmm
QuoteReply
# Mar 07 2002 at 10:58 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592246026912
6 posts
Ummmm... you never played a bard did you? This weapon would be very useful to any bard. IF it truly is a triggered effect bards could dots kill mobs like other casters. It would be very nice to root and twist 3 dots on a mob that total about 100 hp a tick. Even if I have no 2hb skill as a bard the effect is enough for me to want this weapon for its right clickable effect.
▲ Page top
RE: Hmm
QuoteReply
# Mar 25 2002 at 12:34 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591769666028
5 posts
It's a proc, not a clickable effect. Since it can't be clicked and bards have no 2 handed abilities, it's a waste to let a bard get this weapon. Indeed, the shovel should almost always go to paladins and SK's IMO: Warriors and rangers hardly ever use 2 handers, and 50+ casters and healers shouldn't be out there beating on mobs unless they're stupid or suicidal.

Balearic Pureheart
57 Knight of Tunare
Officer, Bloodsong
Lanys T'Vyl
▲ Page top
RE: Hmm
QuoteReply
# Dec 07 2002 at 1:32 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592246024631
50 posts
Warrior's never use 2handers? Where do ya get that idea?

Dual Wielding is excellent for getting taunt, but with the damage bonus, most 2handers will outdamage even a very good dual wield combo.

Any not MT warrior should be using a higher damage, lower aggro 2hander

Even in most of my exp groups I can keep Aggro with a good 2hander, and if root sticks, I just stand super close while others back up a bit to keep aggro.
▲ Page top
RE: Hmm
QuoteReply
# Apr 20 2002 at 11:10 PM Rating: Decent

294 posts
Why does everyone always forget about Monks? :P

This would be an awesome stick for a monk. Looks like a better weapon that the Tranquil staff to me.. 4.5wt compared to 10 weight, I'll take some ratio hits to save 5.5 weight.

QuoteReply
# Feb 21 2002 at 1:15 PM Rating: Decent
Darknyss
Scholar
551 posts
Ok, I've seen people use shovels before, and they always hit things with the stick end...

C'mon... I wanna whack the mob with that big metal digging part and hear that *CLUNK* sound like when I hit suits of Sentient armor or other metal mobs...

If only Verant put a little more thought and creativity into the items as some of the players can come up with...

Pain Lord Darknyss Grimscale

Hey ... this isn't a bad monk item. Nice ratio and reasonable weight. The effect could cause some trouble.

Now all ye need are some stupid battle cries:

"I will dig your grave! Mwahahahah!"

"I shall Club you wit the Ace of Spades!"

"Shovel off your driveway for a dollar, mister?"

Oh-kay ... I'm done ^_^
